workout.title: Chest Day - BODYBUILD
workout.description: Chest Day workout focusing on bodybuild training.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, wrists stacked over elbows and thumbs wrapped around the bar.
Retract and depress the shoulder blades (pinch the shoulder blades together) to create a stable chest platform.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over the chest so it is balanced and steady.
Inhale, lower the bar under control to the mid-chest (sternum) keeping elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso.
Pause briefly with the bar touching or just above the chest while maintaining tightness through the core and legs.
Exhale and drive the bar upward by extending the elbows, pressing the bar back to the start position while keeping wrists neutral.
Avoid excessive elbow flare or bouncing the bar off the chest; maintain a controlled tempo and keep feet planted.
After the set, carefully re-rack the bar by guiding it back onto the bench supports before releasing your grip.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a supine pressing exercise performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also working the triceps and anterior delts. The lifter lowers a barbell to the chest and presses it back to full arm extension under control.

exercises.name: Barbell Incline Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set an adjustable bench to a 30-45 degree incline, lie back with your head, upper back, and glutes firmly against the pad, and plant feet flat on the floor.
instructions: Set an adjustable bench to a 30-45 degree incline, lie back with your head, upper back, and glutes firmly against the pad, and plant feet flat on the floor.
Grip the barbell slightly wider than shoulder-width with palms facing away, unrack the bar and position it directly above your upper chest with arms extended.
Inhale and lower the bar with control to your upper chest near the collarbone, keeping elbows at a 45-60 degree angle from your torso.
Press the bar back up explosively while exhaling, extending your arms fully without locking the elbows aggressively.
Repeat for desired reps, then carefully re-rack the barbell with control.
exercises.description: The barbell incline bench press is a compound pressing movement performed on an inclined bench that emphasizes the upper portion of the pectorals while also engaging the anterior delts and triceps. It is done from a supine position on an incline bench, pressing a barbell vertically from chest to full arm extension.

exercises.name: Barbell Decline Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set a decline bench into a rack and place a barbell at a height you can safely unrack while lying down; secure your feet under the decline foot pads.
instructions: Set a decline bench into a rack and place a barbell at a height you can safely unrack while lying down; secure your feet under the decline foot pads.
Lie supine on the decline bench so your head is lower than your hips and your shoulders and back are flat against the bench; keep feet locked under the pads for stability.
Grasp the bar with a double overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder width (pronated grip); wrists stacked over elbows and shoulders pulled down and back.
Unrack the bar by straightening your arms and moving the bar out over your lower chest; maintain a controlled, tight torso and scapular retraction.
Inhale and slowly lower the bar in a controlled path to the lower chest/upper abdomen (sternal/xyphoid area) with elbows at roughly a 30–45° angle from the torso.
Pause briefly with the bar touching or just above the chest, then exhale and press the bar upward along the same path until arms are nearly extended.
Avoid flaring the elbows excessively and keep the feet anchored under the pads; use a spotter for heavy sets and re-rack the bar carefully after the set.
exercises.description: A decline barbell bench press performed lying supine on a decline bench with the head lower than the hips to emphasize the lower portion of the pectorals. The lifter lowers the bar to the lower chest/upper abdomen and presses it back up to full extension.

exercises.name: Smith Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set the Smith machine bar to an appropriate height, add desired weight, and set safety stops slightly below chest level.
instructions: Set the Smith machine bar to an appropriate height, add desired weight, and set safety stops slightly below chest level.
Position a flat bench in the machine so the bar will line up with the mid-chest when you are lying down.
Lie supine on the bench with feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and a slight natural arch in the low back; retract your shoulder blades to create a stable chest platform.
Grasp the bar with a medium grip (hands slightly wider than shoulder-width) and ensure wrists are stacked over elbows; keep head on the bench.
Unrack the bar by rotating it or disengaging the safety, then lower the bar under control to the mid-chest/sternum while keeping elbows at about a 30–60° angle from the torso.
Pause briefly with the bar lightly touching the chest (do not bounce), then press the bar up explosively until arms are nearly straight without locking the elbows aggressively.
Maintain a tight core and pressed scapulae throughout the movement for stability; avoid excessive flaring of the elbows or hyperextending the lower back.
Control the descent on each rep and re-rack the bar by rotating it back into the safety catches when finished.
exercises.description: A Smith machine variation of the flat bench press performed lying supine on a flat bench, pressing the fixed bar vertically to work the chest. The guided bar path reduces stabilization demand while emphasizing pressing strength in the pectorals.

exercises.name: Smith Incline Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench inside the Smith machine at a 30–45° angle and load the bar with appropriate weight, securing plates with collars.
instructions: Set an incline bench inside the Smith machine at a 30–45° angle and load the bar with appropriate weight, securing plates with collars.
Sit on the bench and lie back so your head, upper back, and buttocks are fully supported (supine on an incline bench); plant feet flat on the floor for stability.
Position yourself so the bar aligns with the upper chest / clavicle area at the bottom of the movement; retract and squeeze your shoulder blades lightly to create a stable pressing platform.
Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width with palms facing away; keep wrists neutral and elbows angled about 30–45° from the torso.
Unrack the bar by rotating the bar hooks or lifting slightly and sliding it out of the safety catches, keeping the motion controlled.
Inhale and lower the bar in a slow, controlled motion to the upper chest (just below the clavicle), keeping the elbows under the bar and the forearms vertical.
Pause briefly at the bottom without bouncing, then exhale and press the bar upward and slightly back toward the rack until arms are nearly extended (do not fully lock the elbows).
Maintain a tight core and do not over-arch the lower back; keep the head and upper back in contact with the bench throughout the set.
After the final repetition, carefully return the bar to the safety hooks by rotating it back into the rack or guiding it onto the stops.
Use safety stops or a spotter as needed and choose a controlled tempo to prioritize form and avoid shoulder strain.
exercises.description: Incline Smith bench press performed on an incline bench in a Smith machine to emphasize the upper portion of the pectorals; the fixed bar path provides stability while pressing from the upper chest toward lockout. Great for building upper chest mass with controlled, guided movement.

exercises.name: Barbell Decline Pullover
exercises.notes: Set a decline bench to a moderate angle and secure your feet under the pads so your body is stable and supine on the bench.
instructions: Set a decline bench to a moderate angle and secure your feet under the pads so your body is stable and supine on the bench.
Grasp a barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip about shoulder-width apart and lift it straight over your chest with elbows slightly bent.
Brace your core and keep your hips down to maintain a neutral spine throughout the movement.
Inhale and slowly lower the barbell in a controlled arc behind your head by hinging at the shoulders; keep a slight bend in the elbows and do not let them flare excessively.
Lower until you feel a deep stretch in the chest/lats or until the bar is roughly level with the crown of your head — do not go so far that your lower back overarches.
Exhale and pull the barbell back in the same arc to the start position over your chest by contracting the pectorals and lats; keep the movement smooth and controlled.
Repeat for the desired reps, maintaining steady tempo and full control on both lowering and lifting phases.
Use a spotter or lighter weight if you cannot control the bar safely behind your head; stop if you feel sharp shoulder or back pain.
exercises.description: Lying supine on a decline bench, the barbell pullover moves the weight in an arc from over the chest to behind the head, emphasizing a deep stretch and contraction of the chest while also engaging the lats and triceps.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Light weight, focus on form and range of motion

warmup.notes: Brief warmup to activate target muscles. Can skip if first working set feels warm.

workout.title: Back Day - BODYBUILD
workout.description: Back Day workout focusing on bodybuild training.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; use an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
Hold the bar with arms extended and shoulders protracted, chest up and eyes looking a few feet in front of you to maintain a neutral neck.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows close to the body (not flaring excessively) and avoid using momentum from the hips; breathe out as you pull.
Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.
Repeat for the desired reps, controlling tempo and keeping the torso stable — if you cannot maintain a flat back, reduce weight or stop and reset.
exercises.description: Standing hip-hinged barbell row where you pull a loaded barbell toward the lower chest/upper abdomen; primarily targets the lats and upper back while requiring posterior chain and core stabilization. Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Lever Pullover (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Step 1: Sit on the machine with your back pressed firmly against the backrest, feet flat on the floor and knees bent at about 90 degrees.
instructions: Step 1: Sit on the machine with your back pressed firmly against the backrest, feet flat on the floor and knees bent at about 90 degrees.
Step 2: Adjust the seat and pivot so the lever handles start just above and behind your head when your arms are extended; select an appropriate weight.
Step 3: Reach up and grasp the handles with a comfortable (typically neutral or slightly pronated) grip, keeping a small, fixed bend in the elbows.
Step 4: Begin with the shoulders stretched overhead and the chest lifted slightly, maintaining a stable torso against the pad.
Step 5: Exhale and pull the lever in a controlled arc down and back toward your hips/upper thighs, leading with the elbows and focusing on squeezing the lats.
Step 6: Pause briefly at the bottom of the movement with elbows by your sides and a full lat contraction; avoid shrugging the shoulders.
Step 7: Inhale and slowly return the lever to the start position, resisting the weight and keeping tension on the lats without letting the shoulders fully relax.
Step 8: Repeat for desired reps; maintain strict form—no torso rocking, keep core engaged, and avoid locking the elbows at the end ranges.
exercises.description: Seated plate-loaded lever pullover that moves the arms in an overhead-to-hip arc to lengthen and contract the lats while also engaging the pectorals and serratus anterior. Performed on a lever machine with the torso supported by a backrest for stability.

exercises.name: Lever T-bar Row (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Set up the lever T-bar station: load plates on the bar, position the foot platform, and select the handle attachment.
instructions: Set up the lever T-bar station: load plates on the bar, position the foot platform, and select the handle attachment. Stand on the platform with feet about hip-width apart.

Hinge at the hips and slightly bend the knees so your torso is angled forward (roughly 30–45 degrees) while keeping a neutral spine and chest up; grip the handles with a double overhand or neutral grip.
Brace your core and retract your shoulder blades before initiating the lift to maintain a stable torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and pull the handle toward your lower chest/upper abdomen by driving the elbows back and squeezing the lats and mid-back, keeping elbows relatively close to the body.
Pause briefly at the top with scapulae squeezed and chest slightly lifted, avoiding excessive leaning back or shrugging the shoulders.
Inhale and lower the handle under control until the arms are nearly fully extended and you feel a stretch in the lats, maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine.
Repeat for the desired reps. Keep tempo controlled, avoid using momentum, and maintain the hinged standing position throughout the set.
exercises.description: A plate-loaded lever T-bar row is a bent-over rowing movement performed from a hinged standing position that primarily targets the lats while engaging the upper back and pulling muscles. It involves pulling a landmine-style bar/handle toward the torso to develop thickness and strength through the mid-back.

exercises.name: Barbell Paused Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (or at a comfortable angled bend); keep the back flat and head in neutral alignment.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (or at a comfortable angled bend); keep the back flat and head in neutral alignment.
Grip the bar with a shoulder-width overhand grip (or mixed grip if needed for heavy loads) and brace your core.
Initiate the movement by pulling your shoulder blades together and driving your elbows back, bringing the bar toward your lower chest/upper abdomen.
Hold and squeeze the shoulder blades together at the top for 1–2 seconds (the paused portion) while keeping the torso stable and avoiding excess torso rotation.
Lower the bar under control until arms are extended and the bar is near knee level or a natural hang, maintaining a neutral spine throughout.
Repeat for desired reps while breathing out during the pull and inhaling as you lower; avoid rounding the back or using excessive momentum.
exercises.description: A bent-over barbell row performed from a hip-hinged position with a brief pause at the top of the pull to maximize scapular retraction and time under tension. Emphasizes rowing the elbows back while keeping the torso stable and the spine neutral.

exercises.name: Barbell Behind The Back Shrug
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, holding a barbell in front of your thighs with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width, arms fully extended.
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, holding a barbell in front of your thighs with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width, arms fully extended.
Keep your chest up, core braced, and maintain a slight bend in the knees with a neutral spine throughout the movement.
Without bending your elbows, shrug your shoulders straight up toward your ears by contracting the traps; avoid rolling the shoulders forward or backward.
Hold the top position for 1-2 seconds, squeezing the traps hard at peak contraction.
Lower the bar slowly and under control back to the starting position, allowing the traps to fully stretch before the next rep.
exercises.description: Standing barbell shrug with the bar held in front of the thighs; raise the shoulders straight up toward the ears to target the upper trapezius, pause at the top, then lower back down under control. This is a fundamental trap-building exercise that emphasizes scapular elevation.

exercises.name: Lever Pronated Grip Seated Scapula Retraction Shrug (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and load the machine so you can reach the handles with your arms extended and feet flat on the footrests; sit upright with a neutral spine and knees bent.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and load the machine so you can reach the handles with your arms extended and feet flat on the footrests; sit upright with a neutral spine and knees bent.
Grasp the handles with a pronated (overhand) grip and let your shoulders relax down and slightly forward to set the start position.
Initiate the movement by pulling the handles back using your shoulder blades: actively retract (pinch) the scapulae together while keeping the torso stable and chest neutral.
As you retract the scapulae, elevate your shoulders into a controlled shrug (shoulder elevation) and squeeze your traps and upper back at the top for 1–2 seconds.
Slowly reverse the motion: lower the shoulders and allow the scapulae to protract back to the start position under control — do not let the weight slam forward.
Keep the arms relatively straight with a slight bend at the elbows, avoid using momentum or torso swing, and breathe out during the contraction and in during the return.
exercises.description: Seated, pronated-grip plate-loaded exercise that emphasizes scapular retraction and an upward shrug to target the trapezius and upper back. Performed from a seated position using the machine handles to actively pinch the shoulder blades together and elevate the shoulders.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

workout.title: Shoulder Day - BODYBUILD
workout.description: Shoulder Day workout focusing on bodybuild training.

exercises.name: Barbell Standing Shoulders Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and knees slightly soft.
instructions: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and knees slightly soft.
Place the barbell in a front-rack position resting on the upper chest/clavicle area with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width and full grip around the bar.
Retract the shoulder blades slightly, keep the chest up and core braced (neutral spine); avoid excessive lumbar arching.
Take a full breath and begin the press by driving the bar upward in a straight line, slightly moving the head back as the bar passes and then returning the head forward under the bar.
Fully extend the elbows at the top with the bar directly over or slightly behind the head, shoulders stacked over hips and feet for balance.
Lower the bar under control back to the front-rack position to complete one rep while inhaling, keeping the elbows tracking slightly forward.
Repeat for desired repetitions, maintaining tight core engagement and controlled tempo; stop or reduce weight if you must excessively lean back to finish reps.
exercises.description: A standing barbell overhead press performed from the front-rack position that primarily develops the delts while requiring the triceps and upper back to assist and stabilize. The lifter presses the barbell from shoulder level to full arm extension overhead while maintaining a braced core.

exercises.name: Barbell Clean and Press
exercises.notes: Drive through your heels, explosively extending hips, knees, and ankles (triple extension) while shrugging shoulders and pulling elbows high.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart, barbell over mid-foot. Grip the bar just outside your knees with an overhand grip, chest up and core braced.
Drive through your heels, explosively extending hips, knees, and ankles (triple extension) while shrugging shoulders and pulling elbows high.
Quickly drop under the bar and rotate elbows forward to catch the barbell in a front-rack position on your shoulders, absorbing with a partial squat.
Stand tall from the front-rack position, then press the bar overhead by extending your arms until elbows are fully locked out.
Lower the bar back to the front-rack position, then return it to the floor with control by hinging at the hips and bending the knees.
exercises.description: A compound Olympic-style lift combining a barbell clean (lifting the bar to a front-rack position) followed immediately by a press overhead, training the shoulders along with legs, hips and upper back. It develops explosive power, pressing strength, and full-body coordination.

exercises.name: Barbell Rear Delt Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips and slightly bend the knees until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (or at a 30–45° angle if mobility requires), keeping a neutral spine and chest up.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and a barbell on the floor in front of you.
Hinge at the hips and slightly bend the knees until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (or at a 30–45° angle if mobility requires), keeping a neutral spine and chest up.
Grip the barbell with an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width so elbows can travel out to the sides.
Brace your core and retract your shoulder blades, maintaining a stable torso throughout the movement.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows outward and up, pulling the bar toward the upper chest/upper abdomen while keeping the elbows high to emphasize the rear delts.
At the top, pause briefly and squeeze the rear delts and upper back without shrugging the shoulders.
Slowly lower the bar under control back to the start position, fully extending the arms while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions; avoid using momentum or excessive torso rotation to lift the weight.
exercises.description: A bent-over barbell row variation that specifically emphasizes the rear delts by rowing with elbows flared out to the sides. Performed from a hip-hinged standing position to target the posterior shoulder and upper back.

exercises.name: Barbell Rear Delt Raise
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart and hold a barbell behind your body with an overhand grip, palms facing away from you.
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart and hold a barbell behind your body with an overhand grip, palms facing away from you.
Keep your chest up, core braced, and maintain an upright posture throughout the movement.
With arms relatively straight, lift the barbell backward and upward away from your body by squeezing the rear delts.
Raise the bar as high as comfortable while maintaining control, then lower slowly back to the starting position.
Avoid leaning forward or using momentum; focus on controlled movement through the rear deltoids.
exercises.description: A standing barbell exercise performed with the bar held behind the body using an overhand grip. The movement involves lifting the barbell backward and upward away from the glutes by extending the shoulders, keeping arms relatively straight to target the posterior deltoids and traps.

exercises.name: Lever Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Set the machine: choose an appropriate weight, adjust the seat height so the pivot of the lever lines up with your shoulder joint and the handles start near your hips.
instructions: Set the machine: choose an appropriate weight, adjust the seat height so the pivot of the lever lines up with your shoulder joint and the handles start near your hips.
Sit upright with your back pressed into the seat pad, feet flat on the floor, and chest up; grasp the handles with a neutral or slightly pronated grip.
Start with your elbows slightly bent and arms resting at your sides (not locked), maintaining a neutral wrist and steady grip.
Exhale and lift the handles out to your sides in a controlled arc, leading with the elbow so the upper arm reaches about parallel to the floor (shoulder height).
Keep your torso still and avoid shrugging your shoulders; use your delts to move the weight rather than momentum or back extension.
Pause briefly at the top with the elbows slightly higher than the wrists, feeling tension in the delts and traps for scapular stability.
Inhale and slowly lower the handles back to the start position with control, maintaining the slight elbow bend.
Repeat for the desired number of reps. Prioritize controlled tempo, full range without swinging, and maintain scapular stability throughout.
exercises.description: Seated lever lateral raise is a machine exercise that isolates the lateral head of the delts by abducting the arms out to the sides from a seated, supported position. It emphasizes controlled movement to raise the arms to about shoulder height while minimizing trunk movement.

exercises.name: Barbell Front Raise
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent, and hold the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip about shoulder-width; let the bar rest against your thighs.
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent, and hold the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip about shoulder-width; let the bar rest against your thighs.
Brace your abs, keep your chest up and spine neutral, and maintain a slight bend in the elbows throughout the movement.
Exhale and raise the barbell straight in front of you by lifting at the shoulders until your arms are roughly parallel to the floor (shoulder height); lead the movement with your elbows.
Hold the top position briefly (0–1 seconds) while keeping the shoulders engaged and avoiding shrugging or excessive leaning back.
Inhale and lower the barbell slowly and under control back to the starting position at your thighs.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, using a weight light enough to avoid swinging or recruiting the legs/back; focus on slow, controlled motion.
exercises.description: A standing barbell front raise that emphasizes the anterior delts by lifting a barbell from the thighs to shoulder height with straight or slightly bent arms while keeping the torso stable to minimize momentum.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

workout.title: Arm Day - BODYBUILD
workout.description: Arm Day workout focusing on bodybuild training.

exercises.name: Barbell Close Grip Bench Press
exercises.notes: Setup: Load the bar and set safety pins or use a spotter.
instructions: Setup: Load the bar and set safety pins or use a spotter. Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight lumbar arch.
Grip: Use a close grip (hands about shoulder-width or slightly narrower — roughly 6–12 inches apart) with a pronated grip, wrists stacked over wrists and the bar.
Scapula and position: Retract and depress your shoulder blades slightly to create a stable chest platform; keep shoulders down away from ears.
Unrack: Drive your feet, take a deep breath, brace your core, lock your elbows to lift the bar out of the rack and position it over your sternum with arms vertical.
Descent: Lower the bar in a controlled manner to the lower chest/sternum while keeping your elbows tucked close to your torso (avoid wide flaring), inhaling as you descend.
Touch and pause: Lightly touch or stop just above the chest (avoid bouncing) and maintain tension in the triceps.
Press: Exhale and press the bar upward by extending the elbows, keeping the bar path slightly diagonal toward the head until near full extension (do not hyperextend or lock aggressively).
Re-rack: After the set, control the bar back to the rack hooks or have a spotter assist. Maintain form on every rep.

Coaching cues & safety: Use a spotter or safety pins for heavy sets, keep wrists neutral, avoid flaring elbows to protect shoulders, and reduce weight if you cannot maintain elbow tuck and controlled descent.
exercises.description: A supine barbell press using a narrow grip that shifts emphasis from the chest to the triceps. Performed on a flat bench, the bar is lowered to the lower chest/sternum with elbows kept close to the torso and then pressed back to lockout.

exercises.name: Barbell Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees soft, and core braced (standing position).
instructions: Stand upright with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees soft, and core braced (standing position).
Hold the barbell with a shoulder-width underhand (supinated) grip so your palms face forward and the bar rests on the base of the fingers.
Start with your arms fully extended down at your thighs, elbows tucked close to your torso and wrists neutral (no excessive bending).
Exhale and curl the bar upward by flexing the elbows, keeping the upper arms and elbows stationary at your sides — lift until the forearms approach the biceps or the bar is near chest level.
Pause briefly at the top and squeeze the biceps, avoiding shoulder shrugging or leaning back (no trunk momentum).
Inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the start position with a controlled eccentric motion until the arms are fully extended.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, maintaining steady tempo and strict form; reduce weight if you cannot keep elbows stable and avoid swinging the torso.
exercises.description: Standing barbell curl performed with a shoulder-width, underhand (supinated) grip; the exercise isolates elbow flexion to work the biceps with the forearms and delts assisting for stabilization. Use a controlled concentric lift and slow eccentric lowering for full muscle stimulation.

exercises.name: Barbell Lying Triceps Extension Skull Crusher
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet planted on the floor for stability and your head near the end of the bench so the bar can be lowered toward your forehead.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet planted on the floor for stability and your head near the end of the bench so the bar can be lowered toward your forehead.
Grip the barbell with a shoulder-width (or slightly narrower) overhand grip and press it up so your arms are fully extended above your chest; wrists should be neutral and stacked over elbows.
Keep your upper arms fixed and perpendicular to the floor (or slightly angled toward your head) throughout the set — only the forearm should move.
Inhale and slowly lower the bar by flexing the elbows, bringing the bar toward your forehead or just above your temples in a controlled arc.
Pause briefly when the bar is a few inches from your forehead (avoid actually hitting your head) and maintain tension in the triceps.
Exhale and extend the elbows to press the bar back up to the start position, locking out but avoiding hyperextension of the elbows.
Keep wrists straight and avoid flaring the elbows outward or letting the upper arms drift; use a spotter if loading heavy for safety.
Perform repetitions with slow, controlled tempo and stop the set if you feel shoulder or wrist pain — reduce weight if form breaks down.
exercises.description: A supine barbell isolation exercise that works elbow extension to load the triceps by lowering the bar toward the forehead (skull crusher) and extending back up. Emphasizes controlled movement from the elbow while keeping the upper arms relatively stationary.

exercises.name: Barbell Preacher Curl
exercises.notes: Reach forward and grasp the barbell with an underhand (supinated) grip about shoulder-width apart, letting your arms fully extend so your elbows are slightly bent and resting on the pad.
instructions: Sit on a preacher bench with your chest and front of your upper arms pressed firmly against the angled pad and feet flat on the floor (seated position).
Reach forward and grasp the barbell with an underhand (supinated) grip about shoulder-width apart, letting your arms fully extend so your elbows are slightly bent and resting on the pad.
Brace your core, keep your torso still and shoulders relaxed (no shrugging), and ensure your wrists remain neutral throughout the movement.
Exhale and curl the barbell upward by flexing the elbows, driving the movement with the biceps; keep the upper arms locked to the pad so only the forearms move.
Pause briefly at the top when the bar is near shoulder level and you feel maximal biceps contraction.
Inhale and slowly lower the barbell back to the start position with controlled tension until your arms are near full extension but not locked out.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining strict form (no swinging or lifting the torso) and using a weight that allows full control.
If wrists or elbows feel strained, reduce weight or use an EZ bar to improve wrist comfort; stop if you feel sharp pain.
exercises.description: Seated barbell preacher curl performed on an angled preacher bench to isolate the biceps by preventing shoulder movement; the upper arms remain supported on the pad while you curl the barbell. Focus is on controlled concentric and eccentric motion to maximize biceps activation.

exercises.name: Barbell Standing Overhead Triceps Extension
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart and knees slightly soft, holding a barbell overhead with a narrow grip (hands close together).
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart and knees slightly soft, holding a barbell overhead with a narrow grip (hands close together).
Press the barbell overhead so it is directly above your head, then slowly tilt the bar back so it can be lowered behind your head while keeping the upper arms stationary and vertical.
Bend only at the elbows to lower the barbell behind your head until forearms approach the back of the upper arms (controlled stretch in the triceps).
Pause briefly at the bottom without moving the upper arms, then exhale and extend the elbows to press the barbell back up to the overhead start position.
Keep elbows pointing forward and close to each other (do not let them flare), brace your abs and avoid arching the lower back throughout the movement.
Control the tempo on both lowering and lifting phases; use a weight that allows strict form and steady breathing.
exercises.description: Standing barbell overhead triceps extension where the lifter lowers a barbell behind the head by flexing the elbows, then extends the elbows to return to the start, isolating the triceps. The shoulders and core remain stable to control movement.

exercises.name: Barbell Prone Incline Curl
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and place it so the top of the bench supports your chest and upper stomach when prone; select an appropriate barbell weight.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and place it so the top of the bench supports your chest and upper stomach when prone; select an appropriate barbell weight.

Lie face down (prone) on the bench with your chest pressed against the pad, legs extended and feet planted on the floor for stability; allow your arms to hang straight down toward the floor holding the barbell with a supinated (underhand) shoulder-width grip.
Retract slightly into the bench so your armpits/upper chest contact the pad and your elbows hang directly beneath your shoulders; keep your torso fixed and avoid shrugging.
Inhale, then curl the barbell by flexing only at the elbows, bringing the bar toward the front of your shoulders while keeping the upper arms stationary against the pad.
Pause briefly at the top with maximal biceps contraction without swinging or using momentum.
Exhale as you slowly lower the barbell back to the fully stretched starting position with controlled tempo, maintaining shoulder and chest contact with the bench.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions; maintain a steady rhythm, avoid letting the wrists collapse, and keep the movement isolated to elbow flexion throughout the set.
exercises.description: Lying prone on an inclined bench with the arms hanging down, you curl a barbell up by flexing the elbows to isolate the biceps. The incline position prevents body swing and increases stretch on the biceps at the bottom of the movement.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Squat
exercises.notes: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height.
instructions: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height. Position yourself under the bar so it rests across your upper traps (high-bar) or rear delts (low-bar) depending on preference.
Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width, elbows pointing down and chest up. Stand with feet roughly shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, then unrack the bar by extending your legs and taking 1–2 controlled steps backward to clear the rack; establish a stable, shoulder-width stance.
Keep a neutral spine and an upright chest. Initiate the movement by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, sending your knees outward to track over your toes.
Descend in a controlled manner until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or deeper if mobility allows) while keeping weight on your midfoot/heels and maintaining a braced core.
Avoid knee collapse (valgus) — consciously push knees outward and keep toes engaged. Do not let the lower back round; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Drive through your heels and midfoot to stand back up, exhaling as you ascend and extending hips and knees simultaneously until fully upright.
Reset your breathing and bracing between reps as needed. When finished, step forward under control and re-rack the bar securely.
Use appropriate weight, a spotter or safety pins in the rack if lifting near maximum loads, and adjust stance or bar position to reduce discomfort in shoulders or lower back.
exercises.description: Barbell back squat is a compound, standing lower-body exercise that primarily develops the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability. The barbell rests across the upper back while you descend by bending hips and knees, then drive back up to standing.

exercises.name: Barbell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Stand upright holding a barbell with an overhand (or mixed) grip, feet about hip-width apart and the bar resting against your thighs; maintain a neutral spine and engaged core.
instructions: Stand upright holding a barbell with an overhand (or mixed) grip, feet about hip-width apart and the bar resting against your thighs; maintain a neutral spine and engaged core.
Set a small, consistent bend in the knees (soft knees) — do not bend into a squat; this is a hinge at the hips, not a knee-dominant lift.
Take a breath in, brace your core, then push your hips back while keeping the chest up and spine neutral; allow the bar to travel down close to the front of your legs.
Lower the bar until you feel a strong but safe stretch in the hamstrings (typically to mid‑shin), keeping the bar close to the body and shoulders pulled back — avoid rounding the lower back.
Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining tension in the hamstrings and back; keep your head in a neutral alignment (looking a few feet in front of you).
Exhale and drive your hips forward by contracting the glutes and hamstrings to return to the standing start position; do not hyperextend the lower back at the top.
Control the descent and ascent on every rep, using a weight that lets you maintain a neutral spine and full hip hinge pattern.
If grip is limiting, switch to a mixed grip or use straps; always prioritize form over load and stop if you feel lumbar rounding or sharp pain.
exercises.description: A bilateral hip-hinge performed from standing that emphasizes the hamstrings by lowering a loaded barbell along the thighs with a neutral spine and slight knee bend; returned to standing by driving the hips forward and squeezing the glutes. Builds posterior chain strength and improves hip extension.

exercises.name: Barbell Glute Bridge (hands on bar)
exercises.notes: Lie supine (on your back) on the floor with knees bent and feet flat about hip-width apart, toes pointed forward.
instructions: Lie supine (on your back) on the floor with knees bent and feet flat about hip-width apart, toes pointed forward.
Roll a loaded barbell over your hips and position the bar across the hip crease; place a pad or towel under the bar for comfort if needed.
Reach down and grasp the bar on either side with both hands (overhand or neutral grip) to stabilize it against your hips; keep elbows tucked and forearms engaged to prevent bar roll.

Brace your core (engage abs), press through your heels, and drive your hips upward by squeezing your glutes until your torso forms a straight line from shoulders to knees; avoid overextending the lower back.
Pause 1–2 seconds at the top while maintaining a tight core and glute contraction, then slowly lower your hips back to the starting position with control.
Breathe out as you lift the hips and inhale as you lower them; keep the bar stable against your hips and avoid letting your knees collapse inward.
Repeat for the desired reps. Use a spotter or safety supports if using heavy loads and ensure the bar is secured with collars.
exercises.description: Supine barbell glute bridge where a loaded barbell rests across the hips and the lifter drives the hips upward by contracting the glutes until the body forms a straight line from shoulders to knees. Hands hold the bar for stability while the core and hamstrings assist the lift.

exercises.name: Lever Sitting Toe Raise
exercises.notes: Place the balls of your feet on the footplate with your heels hanging off the edge and grasp the machine handles or sides of the bench for stability.
instructions: Sit on the machine bench with your knees bent at about 90 degrees and the padded lever resting across your lower thighs just above the knees.
Place the balls of your feet on the footplate with your heels hanging off the edge and grasp the machine handles or sides of the bench for stability.
Start with your heels lowered to feel a stretch in the calves (ankles dorsiflexed).
Exhale and press down through the balls of your feet, plantarflexing the ankles to raise your heels as high as possible while keeping the knees stationary.
Hold the top position for a brief pause, squeezing the calves, then slowly lower your heels back to the start position under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, keeping a controlled tempo and maintaining upright torso and stable knee position throughout.
exercises.description: A seated calf-raise performed on a lever-style machine that isolates the lower leg by plantarflexing the ankle against resistance while the knees are bent. It emphasizes the soleus and gastrocnemius to build calf strength and size.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Crunch (hands pad)
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine weight and seat height so the padded lever rests comfortably across your upper chest/forearms when seated and your feet sit flat on the floor.
instructions: Adjust the machine weight and seat height so the padded lever rests comfortably across your upper chest/forearms when seated and your feet sit flat on the floor.
Sit upright on the seat with knees bent at about 90 degrees, feet shoulder-width apart and planted. Place your forearms/hands on top of the pad or grasp the machine handles if provided.
Start with your torso upright and spine neutral; brace your core and engage the abs before initiating movement.
Exhale and flex the spine, curling your torso forward by bringing your ribcage toward your pelvis. Lead the movement with your sternum rather than pulling with your arms.
Pause for 1 second at the top of the contraction while keeping the motion slow and controlled.
Inhale and slowly return to the upright starting position by extending the spine in a controlled manner; avoid letting the weight drop or using momentum.
Maintain stable hips and legs throughout (do not lift or thrust with the lower body) and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: Seated crunch performed on a lever/pad machine that isolates the abdominal muscles by flexing the torso while the forearms rest on the pad. The movement uses controlled spinal flexion to contract the abs and lift the ribcage toward the pelvis.

exercises.name: Lever Rotary Calf
exercises.notes: Grip the side handles for stability and ensure the cable attachment is secured around the balls of your feet.
instructions: Sit on the machine with your back against the pad and legs extended straight out in front of you; place the balls of your feet against the footplate with heels free to move.
Grip the side handles for stability and ensure the cable attachment is secured around the balls of your feet.
Start with ankles in a neutral or slightly dorsiflexed position to feel a stretch in the calves.
Press through the balls of your feet to plantarflex the ankles, pointing your toes away from you and squeezing the calves at peak contraction.
Slowly return to the starting position with control, allowing a full stretch in the calves before the next rep.
exercises.description: Lever rotary calf is a machine-based calf raise performed from a seated position with legs extended, isolating plantarflexion to target the calf muscles through a controlled range of motion using a cable-pulley system.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Brace your core, pull your shoulders back slightly, and set a neutral spine; keep a small, fixed bend in the knees (soft knees) — do not squat.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell over the middle of your feet; grip the bar just outside your thighs with an overhand or mixed grip.
Brace your core, pull your shoulders back slightly, and set a neutral spine; keep a small, fixed bend in the knees (soft knees) — do not squat.
Initiate the movement by pushing your hips straight back (hip hinge) and allow the bar to descend close to your legs, keeping your chest up and eyes forward.
Continue hinging until you feel a stretch in the hamstrings (bar typically reaches just below the knees or mid-shin depending on flexibility) while maintaining a flat back.
Pause briefly at the bottom without rounding the lower back; keep the lats engaged to keep the bar close to the body.
Drive the hips forward by contracting the glutes and hamstrings to return to a standing position, finishing with hips fully extended but without hyperextending the spine.
Breathe: inhale on the descent, exhale as you drive the hips forward. Repeat for desired repetitions, focusing on controlled tempo and maintaining the hip-hinge pattern.
exercises.description: A hip-hinge movement performed standing with a barbell that emphasizes eccentric and concentric loading of the hamstrings and glutes while the posterior chain and grip stabilize the load. Maintain a neutral spine and a soft knee angle throughout the movement.

exercises.name: Lever Glute Press
exercises.notes: Set the lever machine to the desired weight and adjust the chest pad height so your torso is upright and supported when gripping the handles.
instructions: Set the lever machine to the desired weight and adjust the chest pad height so your torso is upright and supported when gripping the handles.
Stand facing the machine with your chest pressed into the pad, both hands holding the handles for support, and feet on the platform or footpads as required by the machine.
Position the working leg so the foot is centered on the footplate with the knee slightly bent (single‑leg variation) or place both feet if using both legs; keep the non‑working leg clear of the path.
Brace your core (abs) and maintain a neutral spine and fixed chest on the pad; avoid leaning forward or arching the lower back.
Drive the working foot backward and slightly upward by extending the hip, contracting the glute until the leg is nearly straight or you reach a controlled end range of hip extension.
Pause briefly at full contraction, squeezing the glute, then slowly return the lever to the start position with control, resisting the weight rather than letting it snap back.
Complete the prescribed reps, then switch legs and repeat. Keep movement smooth and controlled throughout; reduce range or weight if you feel lumbar strain.
exercises.description: A chest‑supported, single‑leg hip extension performed on a lever machine that drives the footplate backward to extend the hip and contract the glute. It develops unilateral hip power and isolates the glute while the core stabilizes the torso.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Calf Press
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat and foot platform so your knees are roughly at 90 degrees when your feet are on the plate and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Adjust the seat and foot platform so your knees are roughly at 90 degrees when your feet are on the plate and select an appropriate weight.
Sit with your back fully supported against the pad and both feet placed hip-width on the lower edge of the foot platform (balls of feet and toes on the platform, heels free to move).
Grip the handles or seat sides for stability and ensure the safety latch or stops are set before starting.
Unlatch the machine (if applicable) and begin from a neutral/dorsiflexed position with a slight stretch in the calves.
Exhale and press through the balls of your feet by plantarflexing the ankles to raise the platform until your calves are fully contracted; do not use the knees or hips to drive the movement.
Pause briefly at the top and squeeze the calves, then inhale and slowly lower the platform under control until you feel a stretch in the calves (avoid dropping quickly or bouncing).
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then re-engage the safety latch before exiting the machine.
exercises.description: Seated on a lever calf press machine, this exercise uses controlled ankle plantarflexion to raise the foot platform and load the calf muscles. It emphasizes the gastrocnemius/soleus complex while the torso and knees remain supported.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Crunch
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the machine with your chest against the angled chest pad and your feet secured under the lower rollers; adjust the seat so the pad rests across your upper chest/shoulders comfortably.
instructions: Sit upright on the machine with your chest against the angled chest pad and your feet secured under the lower rollers; adjust the seat so the pad rests across your upper chest/shoulders comfortably.
Grasp the handles at chest level (or hold the pad if no handles) and set a moderate weight; maintain a neutral head position and brace your core.

Exhale and contract your abs to curl your torso forward and down, flexing the spine while keeping the hips stationary and using controlled motion — continue until you feel a full contraction of the abs.
Pause briefly at the bottom of the movement while keeping tension in the abs; do not pull with your arms or yank with momentum.
Inhale and slowly return to the starting upright position with a controlled motion, maintaining core engagement and avoiding hyperextension of the spine.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions. Adjust weight to maintain strict form and avoid using neck or hip drive to complete the movement.
exercises.description: A seated, machine-based crunch performed with a chest pad and lever; the exercise flexes the torso forward against resistance to target the rectus abdominis. The knees and feet are stabilized while the upper torso curls forward, emphasizing spinal flexion.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.
instructions: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.

Lie supine on the bench with your head, upper back and glutes in contact with the bench, feet flat on the floor and a small natural arch in the lower back; retract and depress your shoulder blades to create a stable chest platform.
Grip the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists should be stacked over the elbows and forearms vertical when the bar is over the chest.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar out over the chest while keeping the scapulae stable; lock the elbows briefly before starting the set.
Inhale and lower the bar in a controlled manner to the mid-chest/sternum (around nipple level), keeping the elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso to protect the shoulders.
Allow the bar to touch or come very close to the chest without bouncing; maintain tension in the pectorals and keep the feet and glutes braced.
Exhale and press the bar upward explosively while keeping the bar path slightly arced toward the hips until the arms are fully extended but not hyperextended.
Pause briefly at the top with the shoulders stable, then begin the next rep. Maintain consistent form and breathing throughout the set.
Use a spotter or safety pins in the rack for heavy sets, and avoid flaring the elbows excessively or letting the lower back lift off the bench.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a compound pressing movement performed lying supine on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and anterior delts. It involves lowering a loaded barbell to the chest and pressing it back up to full arm extension with controlled technique.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; use an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
Hold the bar with arms extended and shoulders protracted, chest up and eyes looking a few feet in front of you to maintain a neutral neck.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows close to the body (not flaring excessively) and avoid using momentum from the hips; breathe out as you pull.
Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.
Repeat for the desired reps, controlling tempo and keeping the torso stable — if you cannot maintain a flat back, reduce weight or stop and reset.
exercises.description: Standing hip-hinged barbell row where you pull a loaded barbell toward the lower chest/upper abdomen; primarily targets the lats and upper back while requiring posterior chain and core stabilization. Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Barbell Standing Shoulders Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and knees slightly soft.
instructions: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and knees slightly soft.
Place the barbell in a front-rack position resting on the upper chest/clavicle area with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width and full grip around the bar.
Retract the shoulder blades slightly, keep the chest up and core braced (neutral spine); avoid excessive lumbar arching.
Take a full breath and begin the press by driving the bar upward in a straight line, slightly moving the head back as the bar passes and then returning the head forward under the bar.
Fully extend the elbows at the top with the bar directly over or slightly behind the head, shoulders stacked over hips and feet for balance.
Lower the bar under control back to the front-rack position to complete one rep while inhaling, keeping the elbows tracking slightly forward.
Repeat for desired repetitions, maintaining tight core engagement and controlled tempo; stop or reduce weight if you must excessively lean back to finish reps.
exercises.description: A standing barbell overhead press performed from the front-rack position that primarily develops the delts while requiring the triceps and upper back to assist and stabilize. The lifter presses the barbell from shoulder level to full arm extension overhead while maintaining a braced core.

tips: Focus on explosive concentric movements.
Take full rest between sets for neural recovery.
Prioritize form over weight - quality reps build strength.

exercises.name: Barbell Deadlift
exercises.notes: Approach the bar by hinging at the hips and bend the knees until your shins touch the bar; keep hips higher than a squat but lower than full upright.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart with the barbell over the mid-foot and toes pointing slightly out.
Approach the bar by hinging at the hips and bend the knees until your shins touch the bar; keep hips higher than a squat but lower than full upright.
Grip the bar just outside your knees with a double overhand or mixed grip; set shoulders slightly in front of the bar and keep the bar close to your shins.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, keep a neutral (flat) spine and chest lifted — avoid rounding the back.
Drive through the heels and simultaneously extend hips and knees, keeping the bar close to your body and the torso angle changing minimally at first.
Stand tall at the top by finishing hip extension and squeezing the glutes; do not hyperextend the lower back — shoulders should be back but not excessively retracted.
To descend, hinge at the hips first by pushing them back, then bend the knees once the bar passes them, lowering the bar under control to the floor while maintaining a neutral spine.
Reset your breath and core brace at the bottom for the next repetition; maintain consistent foot placement, bar path, and posture throughout the set.
exercises.description: A hip-hinge strength exercise that lifts a loaded barbell from the floor by extending the hips and knees; it targets the posterior chain with heavy involvement of the hamstrings and glutes while the back and core stabilize the torso.

exercises.name: Barbell Hip Thrust
exercises.notes: Plant your feet flat on the floor hip-width apart with knees bent so shins are near vertical; grip the barbell at the sides to steady it.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your upper back (shoulder blades) against a flat bench and roll the barbell over your legs so it rests in the hip crease; use padding on the bar for comfort.
Plant your feet flat on the floor hip-width apart with knees bent so shins are near vertical; grip the barbell at the sides to steady it.
Brace your core, press through your heels and drive your hips upward until your torso and thighs form a straight line; actively squeeze the glutes at the top.
Hold the top briefly while maintaining a neutral spine and avoiding lumbar hyperextension (keep ribs down and chin slightly tucked).
Lower your hips under control until they are just above the floor (or until a full comfortable range) and immediately begin the next rep.
Adjust foot placement as needed (closer to mid-foot emphasizes glutes, higher placement can increase hamstring involvement) and use a spotter when using heavy loads.
exercises.description: A bench-supported hip extension where a loaded barbell is driven by the hips to target glute strength and hypertrophy while the upper back rests on a bench. The movement emphasizes powerful horizontal hip extension with controlled lowering.

tips: Focus on explosive concentric movements.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, wrists stacked over elbows and thumbs wrapped around the bar.
Retract and depress the shoulder blades (pinch the shoulder blades together) to create a stable chest platform.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over the chest so it is balanced and steady.
Inhale, lower the bar under control to the mid-chest (sternum) keeping elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso.
Pause briefly with the bar touching or just above the chest while maintaining tightness through the core and legs.
Exhale and drive the bar upward by extending the elbows, pressing the bar back to the start position while keeping wrists neutral.
Avoid excessive elbow flare or bouncing the bar off the chest; maintain a controlled tempo and keep feet planted.
After the set, carefully re-rack the bar by guiding it back onto the bench supports before releasing your grip.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a supine pressing exercise performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also working the triceps and anterior delts. The lifter lowers a barbell to the chest and presses it back to full arm extension under control.

exercises.name: Barbell Incline Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set an adjustable bench to a 30-45 degree incline, lie back with your head, upper back, and glutes firmly against the pad, and plant feet flat on the floor.
instructions: Set an adjustable bench to a 30-45 degree incline, lie back with your head, upper back, and glutes firmly against the pad, and plant feet flat on the floor.
Grip the barbell slightly wider than shoulder-width with palms facing away, unrack the bar and position it directly above your upper chest with arms extended.
Inhale and lower the bar with control to your upper chest near the collarbone, keeping elbows at a 45-60 degree angle from your torso.
Press the bar back up explosively while exhaling, extending your arms fully without locking the elbows aggressively.
Repeat for desired reps, then carefully re-rack the barbell with control.
exercises.description: The barbell incline bench press is a compound pressing movement performed on an inclined bench that emphasizes the upper portion of the pectorals while also engaging the anterior delts and triceps. It is done from a supine position on an incline bench, pressing a barbell vertically from chest to full arm extension.

exercises.name: Barbell Decline Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set a decline bench in a rack and load the barbell with an appropriate weight; place your feet securely under the decline foot pads.
instructions: Set a decline bench in a rack and load the barbell with an appropriate weight; place your feet securely under the decline foot pads.
Sit on the bench, then lie back so your head is lower than your hips (decline position) and hook your feet under the pads to prevent sliding.
Grip the bar with a slightly wider-than-shoulder-width overhand grip, wrists stacked over elbows and shoulder blades retracted and depressed.
Unrack the bar by straightening your arms and moving the bar over your lower chest/upper sternum; have a spotter if lifting heavy.
Inhale, then lower the bar in a controlled manner to touch or come just above the lower chest/upper abdomen (lower sternum), keeping elbows at about a 45-degree angle to the torso.
Pause briefly at the bottom without bouncing, then exhale and press the bar upward by extending the elbows until the arms are nearly straight (do not lock aggressively).
Maintain tightness through the chest and core, keep the scapulae retracted, and avoid excessive arching of the lower back throughout the set.
Control the descent on each rep and re-rack the bar safely with the help of a spotter when finished.
exercises.description: A barbell pressing movement performed on a decline bench that emphasizes the lower portion of the pectorals. Lying with the head lower than the hips, you lower the bar to the lower chest/upper abdomen and press it back up.

exercises.name: Barbell Reverse Grip Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on the floor with legs extended or knees bent, holding the barbell with a reverse (underhand/supinated) grip at about shoulder-width apart.
instructions: Lie supine on the floor with legs extended or knees bent, holding the barbell with a reverse (underhand/supinated) grip at about shoulder-width apart.
Position the bar directly over your chest with arms extended, keeping wrists stacked over elbows.
Lower the bar in a controlled manner until your upper arms lightly touch the floor, keeping elbows at roughly 45 degrees to your torso.
Pause briefly when arms contact the floor, then press the bar back up by extending the elbows until arms are fully extended.
Keep your core braced throughout and exhale during the pressing phase; use a spotter when handling heavier loads.
exercises.description: A floor press performed with a supinated (underhand/reverse) grip that places greater emphasis on the upper chest while still engaging the triceps and front delts. Performed lying supine on the floor with legs extended, the bar is lowered until the upper arms touch the floor, then pressed back to full arm extension. The floor limits range of motion, reducing shoulder stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Reverse Grip Bench Press on Floor
exercises.notes: Lie flat on the floor with legs extended and grasp the barbell with a reverse (supinated/underhand) grip at shoulder-width.
instructions: Lie flat on the floor with legs extended and grasp the barbell with a reverse (supinated/underhand) grip at shoulder-width.
Unrack or lift the bar to position it directly over your chest with arms fully extended.
Lower the bar slowly until your upper arms touch or nearly touch the floor, keeping elbows tucked at about 45 degrees.
Press the bar back up to full arm extension, squeezing the chest at the top.
Maintain a flat back against the floor throughout the movement and control the tempo on each rep.
exercises.description: A floor-based bench press performed with a reverse (underhand/supinated) grip that emphasizes the upper portion of the pectorals while limiting range of motion. Lying on the floor, you lower the bar to the chest/upper torso and press it back up under control.

tips: Focus on explosive concentric movements.
Take full rest between sets for neural recovery.
Prioritize form over weight - quality reps build strength.

exercises.name: Smith Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set the Smith machine bar to an appropriate height, add desired weight, and set safety stops slightly below chest level. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set the Smith machine bar to an appropriate height, add desired weight, and set safety stops slightly below chest level.
Position a flat bench in the machine so the bar will line up with the mid-chest when you are lying down.
Lie supine on the bench with feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and a slight natural arch in the low back; retract your shoulder blades to create a stable chest platform.
Grasp the bar with a medium grip (hands slightly wider than shoulder-width) and ensure wrists are stacked over elbows; keep head on the bench.
Unrack the bar by rotating it or disengaging the safety, then lower the bar under control to the mid-chest/sternum while keeping elbows at about a 30–60° angle from the torso.
Pause briefly with the bar lightly touching the chest (do not bounce), then press the bar up explosively until arms are nearly straight without locking the elbows aggressively.
Maintain a tight core and pressed scapulae throughout the movement for stability; avoid excessive flaring of the elbows or hyperextending the lower back.
Control the descent on each rep and re-rack the bar by rotating it back into the safety catches when finished.
exercises.description: A Smith machine variation of the flat bench press performed lying supine on a flat bench, pressing the fixed bar vertically to work the chest. The guided bar path reduces stabilization demand while emphasizing pressing strength in the pectorals.

exercises.name: Lever Pullover (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Step 1: Sit on the machine with your back pressed firmly against the backrest, feet flat on the floor and knees bent at about 90 degrees. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Step 1: Sit on the machine with your back pressed firmly against the backrest, feet flat on the floor and knees bent at about 90 degrees.
Step 2: Adjust the seat and pivot so the lever handles start just above and behind your head when your arms are extended; select an appropriate weight.
Step 3: Reach up and grasp the handles with a comfortable (typically neutral or slightly pronated) grip, keeping a small, fixed bend in the elbows.
Step 4: Begin with the shoulders stretched overhead and the chest lifted slightly, maintaining a stable torso against the pad.
Step 5: Exhale and pull the lever in a controlled arc down and back toward your hips/upper thighs, leading with the elbows and focusing on squeezing the lats.
Step 6: Pause briefly at the bottom of the movement with elbows by your sides and a full lat contraction; avoid shrugging the shoulders.
Step 7: Inhale and slowly return the lever to the start position, resisting the weight and keeping tension on the lats without letting the shoulders fully relax.
Step 8: Repeat for desired reps; maintain strict form—no torso rocking, keep core engaged, and avoid locking the elbows at the end ranges.
exercises.description: Seated plate-loaded lever pullover that moves the arms in an overhead-to-hip arc to lengthen and contract the lats while also engaging the pectorals and serratus anterior. Performed on a lever machine with the torso supported by a backrest for stability.

exercises.name: Smith Shoulder Press
exercises.notes: Set an adjustable bench to an upright (near 80–90°) position inside the Smith machine and set the bar height so it rests at about upper-chest / shoulder level when seated. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set an adjustable bench to an upright (near 80–90°) position inside the Smith machine and set the bar height so it rests at about upper-chest / shoulder level when seated.
Sit with your back firmly against the pad, feet flat on the floor, core braced and head neutral. Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width with a full grip.
Unrack the bar by rotating the safety hooks (or lifting slightly and disengaging the catches) so the bar is free to move on the Smith rails.
Inhale, lower the bar under control to about collarbone/upper-chest or just below shoulder level, keeping elbows slightly forward and wrists stacked over elbows.

Exhale and press the bar upward in a vertical path until your arms are nearly fully extended (avoid locking the elbows aggressively), keeping your back pressed to the bench and avoiding excessive spinal arch.
Slowly lower the bar back to the start position with control and repeat for the desired repetitions.
Rack the bar safely by returning it to the catches before releasing your grip. Adjust weight or grip width to maintain good form and avoid shoulder pain.
exercises.description: Seated overhead press performed on a Smith machine that drives a vertical bar path to target the shoulder (delts). The movement presses the bar upward from shoulder height to full arm extension while the bench back provides support.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
Use slow, controlled tempo throughout the movement.
Allow full recovery between exercises - this is not cardio.
